# abuse

```go
import "github.com/its-ernest/echox/abuse"
```

Package abuse provides behavioral analysis middleware for Echo v5. It tracks client reputation via a scoring system to mitigate scrapers and scanners.

## Index

- [Variables](<#variables>)
- [func New\(config Config\) echo.MiddlewareFunc](<#New>)
- [type Config](<#Config>)
- [type Rule](<#Rule>)


## Variables

<a name="DefaultConfig"></a>

```go
var DefaultConfig = Config{
    Threshold: 100,
    Cooldown:  30 * time.Minute,
    ErrorHandler: func(c *echo.Context, score int) error {
        return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusForbidden, "Access denied due to suspicious activity")
    },
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
## func New

```go
func New(config Config) echo.MiddlewareFunc
```

New returns an Echo middleware that monitors and limits abusive behavior. It should be placed early in the middleware stack to protect downstream resources.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
e := echo.New()

// Instant-ban on sensitive files, warn on API spam
e.Use(abuse.New(abuse.Config{
	Threshold: 100,
	Rules: []abuse.Rule{
		{Path: "/.env", Score: 100},
		{Path: "/api/v1/*", Score: 5},
	},
}))
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Config"></a>
## type Config

Config defines the settings for the abuse detection middleware.

```go
type Config struct {
    Store     store.Counter
    Threshold int
    Rules     []Rule
    Cooldown  time.Duration
    // EnableDelay adds latency to suspicious requests instead of a hard block.
    EnableDelay  bool
    ErrorHandler func(c *echo.Context, score int) error
}
```

<a name="Rule"></a>
## type Rule

Rule defines the "heat" points added for specific path patterns. Pattern supports suffix wildcards like "/admin\*".

```go
type Rule struct {
    Path  string
    Score int
}
```
